data.description:
## Context

The CI workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml:199-205 currently runs LLM review as a single claude-code-action call with the prompt "Review this pull request for correctness, security issues, and design problems." /dso:review locally dispatches multi-tier classifier-driven reviewers with parallel overlays, JSON-schema-validated findings, and integrity-verified hash. The two are not equivalent — CI is dramatically lighter (1 LLM call vs up to 8 with overlays). Projects with enforcement.strategy=ci (introduced by the follow-on epic) currently get a fidelity downgrade. This epic ports /dso:review's architecture into CI so the parity assumption underlying that follow-on epic actually holds. Additionally, because the DSO plugin's agent files and helper scripts live in the plugin repo (not in host project repos), this epic introduces a 3-tier version resolution + fetch mechanism so host-project CI can access the plugin assets at runtime.

## Success Criteria

- CI llm-review invokes review-complexity-classifier.sh against the PR diff to select tier per PR; DSO_LLM_TIER env var removed.
- Named agent files (code-reviewer-{light,standard,deep-correctness,deep-verification,deep-hygiene,deep-arch}.md) loaded verbatim as system prompt for each claude-code-action invocation; classifier output drives which agents fire.
- Deep tier dispatches 3 parallel sonnet specialists + 1 opus arch synthesis (4 LLM calls, evidenced by reviewer-findings.json containing entries from all four agents); light/standard 1 each.
- Overlay flags from classifier (security/performance/test-quality) auto-dispatch corresponding overlay agents in parallel.
- Findings written via write-reviewer-findings.sh, verified by record-review.sh with --reviewer-hash; non-zero exit on score < 4 or critical finding.
- Test PR with deliberate violation (e.g., hardcoded API secret in a tracked file) triggers security overlay and CI exits non-zero with a SECURITY_OVERLAY_TRIGGERED finding in the structured artifact — verified in-session.
- CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md and onboarding/SKILL.md updated to remove all DSO_LLM_TIER references and document the new classifier-driven tier selection plus the new ci.dso_plugin_version config key; if any CI job is renamed, .github/required-checks.txt is updated and validate-required-checks.sh passes — verified in-session.
- Generated host-project CI workflow includes a Resolve-and-fetch DSO plugin assets step that walks a 3-tier version resolution chain via new helper plugins/dso/scripts/resolve-dso-version.sh: tier 1 reads the project's Claude per-project plugin-tracking file (canonical filename resolved at implementation time and documented in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md as part of this SC); tier 2 reads ci.dso_plugin_version in .claude/dso-config.conf; tier 3 fetches marketplace.json from navapbc/digital-service-orchestra@HEAD and reads the stable dso channel only (resolver MUST NOT fall back to dso-dev — rejects with explicit error if stable channel is absent or malformed). Resolved version + source tier is logged at INFO before clone. Clone targets ${RUNNER_TEMP:-\$(mktemp -d)}/dso/<version>/ with per-version subdir to prevent cross-job staleness; mktemp fallback when RUNNER_TEMP is unset on non-GHA CI. Clone success is gated by a sentinel file written only after fetch completes; existence-check uses the sentinel, not the directory. Fail-closed: all-three-tiers-fail exits non-zero with diagnostic indicating which tier(s) failed.
- ci-llm-review-runner.sh auto-detects mode: when plugins/dso/agents/ is present at \$REPO_ROOT it reads from local checkout; else reads from \$DSO_ASSETS_DIR (env var set by the fetch step). Both paths covered by tests.
- Host-project E2E verified end-to-end against a fresh project bootstrapped via create-dso-app.sh in \$RUNNER_TEMP during the verification session, with .claude/dso-config.conf manually patched to set merge.strategy=pr — a deliberate-violation test PR triggers the security overlay and blocks merge.

## Dependencies

None. Blocks the PR-merge mode + enforcement-strategy config epic and the operational flip epic (re-scoped 1083-fb3d).

## Approach

New plugins/dso/scripts/ci-llm-review-runner.sh orchestrates: compute-diff-hash → review-complexity-classifier.sh → tier-branched parallel claude-code-action calls per agent slot → overlay dispatch in parallel → record-review.sh final assertion. Reuses existing agent files in plugins/dso/agents/ verbatim as claude-code-action system prompts. New plugins/dso/scripts/resolve-dso-version.sh walks the 3-tier version chain. The CI workflow generated for host projects (via Epic A's ci-generator.sh --mode=pr-protected) includes a Resolve-and-fetch step that runs resolve-dso-version.sh, clones the DSO plugin to a per-version subdir under RUNNER_TEMP (with mktemp fallback), and exports DSO_ASSETS_DIR for the runner.

## Scenario Analysis

- Parallel claude-code-action calls write reviewer-findings.json concurrently, causing partial overwrites (critical, runtime).
- claude-code-action step timeout kills mid-parallel-batch, leaving reviewer-findings.json in inconsistent state (high, runtime).
- review-complexity-classifier.sh produces empty or malformed tier output when diff is binary-only or exceeds size limit (high, runtime).
- Diff hash computed from a different base than the LLM agents analyze, causing --reviewer-hash mismatch on every CI run (critical, runtime).
- First CI run on a repo without DSO_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret blocks all PRs with opaque auth failure (high, deployment).
- Named agent .md files not present at expected path in the CI runner filesystem, causing runner to fall back to inline prompt (high, deployment).
- ci.yml job name drift from required-checks.txt causes branch protection to stop enforcing the new llm-review job (critical, deployment).
- Parallel claude-code-action invocations exceed GitHub Actions concurrency limits or Anthropic rate limits, causing intermittent job failures (high, deployment).
- Missing or malformed dso-config.conf on first-time CI run causes ci-llm-review-runner.sh to silently skip overlay dispatch (critical, configuration).
- Classifier scores a 300+ line diff below the opus-upgrade threshold, but the upgrade threshold is hardcoded in ci-llm-review-runner.sh rather than read from config (medium, configuration).
- Clone race between concurrent CI jobs fetching same DSO version on shared self-hosted runner — second clone fails 'destination exists', fail-closed propagates as review-gate failure rather than transient infra error (high, runtime).
- Network timeout during plugin clone leaves partial checkout; re-run detects directory and skips re-cloning, runner reads corrupt or missing agent files (high, runtime).
- Tier 1 version present in project Claude config but tag has been deleted from upstream — chain doesn't fall through silently, diagnostic doesn't indicate fallback was skipped (high, configuration).
- ci.dso_plugin_version=<empty> short-circuits fallback to tier 3 — empty value treated as set (medium, configuration).
- marketplace.json tier-3 falls through to dev channel HEAD if stable dso channel entry is misconfigured/absent, producing non-reproducible CI runs (high, configuration).
- RUNNER_TEMP undefined on non-GitHub-Actions CI (Jenkins/GitLab/CircleCI); $RUNNER_TEMP/dso expands to /dso/, requires root, fails (high, deployment).
- $RUNNER_TEMP/dso/ from a prior job survives into jobs targeting a different version on the same runner — subsequent matrix job resolves N+1 but skips re-clone, reads stale agent files (high, deployment).

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (claude-code-action; GitHub Actions; Anthropic API), CI/CD workflow changes
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 17 (10 initial + 7 from distribution-mechanism revision)
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: scenario analysis re-run 1 time (after distribution-mechanism revision)
- Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0): not triggered
- Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5): not triggered (architectural primitives all exist locally)
- LLM-instruction signal (Step 5): triggered — keyword: agent definitions
- Scale context (Step 0): small scale (default) — per-PR / per-CI-job invocation; not volume-sensitive
data.title: CI LLM-review parity: port /dso:review classifier, agents, overlays, and integrity into CI; 3-tier version resolution for distribution
